comments.text: Is it possible that there is a typo in this question? My understanding is that "largest m" would collapse to the trivial answer m = n, which doesn't line up with the rest of the page. Is this supposed to be "smallest m"? (The site has been updated to address this comment.)
Yes, this is a typo, this should be smallest $m$, thanks.

I will note that a resolution to this problem would follow from "hypergraph Nikiforov". conjecture: for any $r$-uniform hypergraph $H$ and $c>0$, there exists $\delta>0$ so that if an $n$-vertex hypergraph $G$ contains $cn^{|V(H)|}$ copies of $H$, then you can find a $t$-blowup of $H$ inside $G$ with $t>\delta (\log n)^{1/(r-1)}$. the partial result of Conlon, Fox, and Sudakov works by observing that standard hypergraph Turan finds a $t$ blowup with $t>\delta(\log n)^{1/(|V(H)|-1)}$.
